what_it_does: Implements the ViIdentity React overlay: a live mirror-application that renders the MS_IDENTITY registry as editable aspects (overview, composed prompt, facets, awareness, memory, dials, kinds, streams, leans, artifacts, actions, behaviours, network) and provides a persistent Vi chat console whose suggested actions write back through the same registries.
observations: Uses window.* globals for all registries, stores, and shared components; no ES imports are visible.
IdentityProjection switches on aspect.projection to dispatch to the appropriate renderer.
Reuses existing surfaces: MemoryAspect, DSSpecimen, KindSpecimen, StreamRunner, and MS_VI_BRAIN sections.
Exports shared renderers window.CandidateCard and window.ArtifactContent, plus window.ViOverview.
IDConsole is a persistent chat column that knows the current aspect and can apply registry mutations via chat.apply.
IDOverview is a reusable live dashboard with counts, pending candidates, recent leans, and quick actions.
IDNetwork visualizes registry nodes in a circle with live counts and doubles as navigation.
Many components call window.useXStore() at the top purely to trigger re-renders on registry changes.
CustomEvent 'vi-navigate' and 'vi-stream-complete' are used for cross-surface routing and stream completion.
Artifact content is rendered generically by scanning object keys for headline/line/list fields and detecting hex colors.
standouts: Persistent Vi chat column alongside every aspect that is context-aware and can mutate registry state through one-tap actions.
Single shared CandidateCard + ArtifactContent used across console, generated lists, and artifact groups to avoid divergent rendering.
IDOverview exported as window.ViOverview so any host surface can embed the same live dashboard.
conventions: React functional components with local useState/useEffect aliased as useStateID/useEffectID.
Global registry API accessed through window.MS_* and window.use*Store hooks.
Component names prefixed with ID for identity-specific surfaces.
Aspect-driven projection via a switch on aspect.projection.
Inline event handlers and deeply nested JSX.
Store hooks invoked unconditionally for subscription side effects.
Fallback to null or empty state when expected globals are absent.
concerns: Heavy reliance on window globals without imports makes static analysis, testing, and SSR fragile.
Duplicate local function names (add, save) across components can confuse stack traces and search.
Some computations assume globals exist without guards (e.g., window.MS_VARY, window.MS_RESOLVE in IDOverview).
IDConsole's read prop is computed from the existence of I.get('makeables'), which is an unusual signal.
Spreading ftarget(aspect) onto a div assumes a prop contract that is not defined in this file.
Artifact status cycling uses a hardcoded mapping that may not cover all statuses.
notes: A large React overlay that turns Vi's identity registry into an inspectable, editable, conversational control panel while reusing existing studio components.
questions: What are the exact shapes and methods of MS_IDENTITY, MS_VI_BRAIN, MS_PRODUCE, MS_STREAMS, MS_RESOLVE, MS_VARY, MS_KINDS, MS_DECISIONS, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_FEEDBACK, MS_MEMORY, MS_CONTEXT, and MS_DATA?
Where are ViMark, Icon, Btn, Eyebrow, EntityShape, FeedbackLayer, ViThread, KindSpecimen, DSSpecimen, MemoryAspect, and StreamRunner defined?
How is useViChat implemented and what is the contract for chat.apply?
What is the intended prop contract for the feedback target object returned by ftarget and spread onto a div?
How are tweaks structured and how does setTweak propagate across the studio?
What is the full lifecycle of contentStatus on artifacts and how does MS_PRODUCE.refine update contentPrev?
How do builtin versus custom behaviours differ in the identity store?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to render MS_IDENTITY as a live mirror-app that composes existing surfaces and writes back to registries; the file does exactly that, adding a persistent chat console and shared candidate/artifact renderers.
apparent_themes: transparent AI: show the prompt and provenance
intention_signals: Make Vi's identity inspectable, editable, and steerable from one overlay; let the user talk to Vi about the exact aspect they are viewing and have Vi's suggested actions mutate the same registries; avoid rebuilding existing UI surfaces by projecting them.
novelty: A persistent conversational column that is context-aware of the current identity aspect and can perform registry mutations via one-tap actions; a unified CandidateCard/ArtifactContent shared renderer used across console and composition; a registry network map that doubles as live navigation.
contribution: Provides a reusable, aspect-driven identity overlay shell, a shared artifact/candidate card renderer, a persistent context-aware Vi chat control, and a live dashboard (ViOverview) that any host surface can embed.
summary_for_embedding: React component file implementing ViIdentity, an overlay that renders the MS_IDENTITY registry as editable aspects, composes existing studio surfaces, and includes a persistent Vi chat console and shared artifact/candidate rendering utilities.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a single, conversational, live mirror for inspecting and editing Vi's identity, behaviours, memory, decisions, and generated artifacts without duplicating existing UI.

symbols.does: Persistent Vi chat column that renders the conversation thread and quick actions.
symbols.description: Reads MS_VI_BRAIN and useViChat, displays messages via ViThread, provides quick jump buttons, an input field, and applies Vi-suggested actions through chat.apply; it knows the current aspect via aspectTitle and can route the user to the changed surface.

symbols.does: Composes a context-aware greeting sentence for the chat column.
symbols.description: Uses brain.where to name the current project/context and invites the user to ask questions or request changes to the active identity aspect.

symbols.does: Sends a user message through the Vi chat hook.
symbols.description: Trims the input (or passed text) and calls chat.send with withActions:true, then clears the local input state.

symbols.does: Executes a Vi-proposed chat action and routes to the relevant aspect.
symbols.description: Handles stream, generate, facet, and behaviour verbs by invoking chat.apply with setTweak, then jumps to artifacts, disposition, or behaviours so the user sees the effect.

symbols.does: Form for teaching Vi a new standing learning.
symbols.description: Lets the user pick a learning kind and scope, enter text, commit it via MS_FEEDBACK.addLearning, and view or remove recently added learnings.

symbols.does: Persists a new learning from the IDTeach form.
symbols.description: Validates the text, calls MS_FEEDBACK.addLearning with the selected scope and kind, shows a toast, and clears the form.

symbols.does: Live dashboard that projects registry state into actionable tiles and candidate decisions.
symbols.description: Subscribes to artifact, feedback, memory, and identity stores; shows counts, the current look, pending candidates needing approval, recent leans, quick actions, and recent activity; exported as window.ViOverview.

symbols.does: Computes a summary of variant biases Vi has learned.
symbols.description: Maps over window.MS_VARY, finds matching kind labels in feedback prefer signals, resolves the current variant via MS_RESOLVE, and returns the leaned value per kind.

symbols.does: Displays the live composed system prompt sections.
symbols.description: Calls read(ctx) to get prompt sections, counts words, labels the model, and renders each section with a link to its source aspect so the user sees the prompt change in real time.

symbols.does: Lists facet cards for identity zones.
symbols.description: Determines the active zone from ctx or MS_DATA and renders a FacetCard for each facet returned by read(ctx).

symbols.does: Editable card for a single identity facet/disposition.
symbols.description: Shows the facet's shape, label, active state, disposition text, and leans; lets the user edit and save the disposition, add or remove leans, and reset edited facets to default.

symbols.does: Persists an edited facet disposition.
symbols.description: Trims the edited text and calls MS_IDENTITY.setFacet, then exits edit mode and notifies the user that the disposition is rewritten.

symbols.does: Adds a new lean string to a facet.
symbols.description: Appends the trimmed lean to the facet's leans array via MS_IDENTITY.setFacet and clears the input.

symbols.does: Removes a lean string from a facet by index.
symbols.description: Filters the leans array and updates the facet through MS_IDENTITY.setFacet.

symbols.does: Renders context rows describing what Vi is aware of.
symbols.description: Maps read(ctx) rows into icon+label+text rows, or shows an empty state if no context exists.

symbols.does: Operable memory recall grouped by topic.
symbols.description: Subscribes to memory store, lets the user add a remembered item with topic and content, adjust weight, or forget items; groups items by topic.

symbols.does: Adds a new memory item.
symbols.description: Validates content, calls MS_MEMORY.learn with origin 'stated', optional topic, and weight 1.2, then clears inputs and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Renders decision dials using DSSpecimen and setTweak.
symbols.description: For each decision id, fetches the decision from MS_DECISIONS, finds the active option, and renders selectable options that call setTweak when chosen.

symbols.does: Generic renderer for an artifact's generated content object.
symbols.description: Reads live artifact state, handles generating/error/pending statuses, picks headline/line/list fields heuristically, supports color swatches, and can toggle between current and previous content when refining; exported as window.ArtifactContent.

symbols.does: Detects whether a string looks like a hex color.
symbols.description: Returns true if the trimmed string matches a hex color pattern, used to render color swatches in ArtifactContent.

symbols.does: Extracts a display label from a list item.
symbols.description: Handles strings, numbers, and objects, preferring label/title/name/headline/screen/step or the first string value.

symbols.does: Extracts secondary text from a list item.
symbols.description: Returns a value, role, body, action, sub, or hex field from an object, or an empty string otherwise.

symbols.does: Compact inline control for refining artifact content.
symbols.description: Toggles an input that calls onRefine with the user's steer when submitted, allowing Vi to regenerate content in place.

symbols.does: Surface for generating parts from makeable kinds.
symbols.description: Subscribes to memory/feedback/artifact stores, resolves each kind against the live project, renders KindSpecimen previews, and creates candidate artifacts via MS_PRODUCE or MS_ARTIFACTS; shows generated CandidateCards with trace and refine.

symbols.does: Creates a candidate artifact for a selected kind.
symbols.description: Uses MS_PRODUCE.create (falling back to MS_ARTIFACTS.create) with the generated context, prepends the result to the local made list, and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Lists and runs guided sequences inline.
symbols.description: Renders available streams as run buttons; when running, mounts StreamRunner with the current site context and setTweak, and returns to the list on exit.

symbols.does: Visualizes accumulated variant preferences per kind.
symbols.description: Computes variant bias and prefer/avoid signals for each kind, separates taught from default leans, and lets the user clear the learnings that shaped a lean.

symbols.does: Clears learnings that mention a kind's label.
symbols.description: Removes active prefer/avoid learnings whose text includes the kind label, then notifies the user.

symbols.does: Shows generated artifacts grouped by kind with status advancement.
symbols.description: Subscribes to artifacts, groups by kind, shows a banner when a stream just produced parts, and lets the user cycle each artifact through a status mapping.

symbols.does: Renders teach/feedback/iterate action items.
symbols.description: Deduplicates actions by id and displays their icon, label, and group tag.

symbols.does: Full CRUD surface for standing behaviours.
symbols.description: Subscribes to identity store, shows active behaviour count, lets the user add new rules, and renders each behaviour via BehaviourRow.

symbols.does: Adds a new standing behaviour.
symbols.description: Validates the rule text, calls store.addBehaviour with an optional label, clears the form, and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Editable row for a single behaviour.
symbols.description: Displays label, active toggle, builtin/custom/edited flags, inline text editing, reset for edited builtins, and delete for custom rules.

symbols.does: Persists edited behaviour text.
symbols.description: Calls store.setBehaviourText with the trimmed text and exits edit mode.

symbols.does: Visualizes the registry network around Vi.
symbols.description: Arranges registry nodes in a circle around a central Vi shape, draws connecting lines, shows live counts per registry, and navigates to the corresponding app page or identity aspect when clicked.

symbols.does: Computes circular layout coordinates for a network node.
symbols.description: Distributes nodes evenly around a 38-unit radius circle starting at -90 degrees and returns percentage x/y positions.

symbols.does: Returns a live count for a registry node.
symbols.description: Looks up counts for MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_MEMORY, MS_FEEDBACK, MS_DECISIONS, MS_KINDS, MS_STREAMS, and MS_CONTEXT via their global APIs, swallowing errors.

symbols.does: Navigates from a network node to its source surface.
symbols.description: When the network is big and clickable, dispatches vi-navigate for page registries or calls onJump for aspect registries.

symbols.does: Handles completion of a guided stream.
symbols.description: Listens for vi-stream-complete, captures produced ids, clears the running stream, and switches to the artifacts aspect with a banner.

symbols.does: Routes a console action to stream running.
symbols.description: If the action type is stream, stores the stream id and selects the streams aspect.

symbols.does: Changes the active identity aspect.
symbols.description: Clears any running stream unless jumping to streams, then updates the selected aspect state.

symbols.does: Builds a feedback target object for the aspect header.
symbols.description: If useFeedback is available, returns a target descriptor scoped to the current aspect so the header can be right-click taught; otherwise returns an empty object.
